items.title: Whistleblower lawsuit accuses Alignment of accounting fraud
items.reason: A whistleblower lawsuit against a Medicare Advantage insurer alleging accounting fraud and financial misstatement is directly relevant to payer litigation and enforcement activities that affect revenue-cycle operations and payer financial health.
items.summary: A former executive filed a whistleblower lawsuit against Alignment Healthcare, a Medicare Advantage insurer, alleging the company improperly recorded millions of dollars in operating expenses as capital expenditures to artificially inflate its financial value. Alignment denied the allegations. This accounting fraud claim could trigger regulatory investigation and financial penalties, potentially affecting the insurer's stability and payment practices for providers. Revenue-cycle teams should monitor this case as it may impact claim processing, contract negotiations, and payer solvency.

items.title: CVS' Omnicare unit agrees to $440M settlement with DOJ in ongoing fraud case
items.reason: This reports a significant DOJ enforcement action and settlement against a major healthcare entity (CVS/Omnicare) involving fraud allegations and a substantial penalty, which directly falls under regulatory enforcement and sanctions involving payers/health insurers.
items.summary: CVS Health and its Omnicare unit agreed to pay $440 million to the Department of Justice as a partial settlement of a nearly $950 million fraud judgment in an ongoing case. Omnicare, a long-term care pharmacy benefit manager and part of the CVS enterprise, has faced ongoing DOJ scrutiny over fraud allegations. This enforcement action represents a significant regulatory sanction against a major healthcare entity that operates in the payer/pharmacy benefit space, impacting how such entities manage claims and billing practices. The settlement reflects ongoing accountability pressures on large healthcare companies for compliance and claims integrity.
